Choosing Between Two Evils: Greg Chappell vs John Buchanan

Sourav Ganguly answered that he would pick the former Indian head coach Greg Chappell.

He said Greg Chappell at least had a working knowledge of the game, while Buchanan didn’t even have that.
This was during his time with KKR, after his famous clash with Chappell back in 2005. [Adda with GB]

2069601653813305747.full_text:
Ganguly Reveals Chappell Wanted to Coach KKR Under His Captaincy

Sourav Ganguly said, “He had also sent me an email once before, in 2011 when I was the Kolkata Knight Riders captain, that he wanted to coach KKR.

When asked how the dynamic would have played out had Ganguly accepted Chappell’s offer? 

Ganguly replied . “Dada is not that big of a fool He might be a fool once, but he can’t be fooled over and over again,” [Adda with GB]

2069647524860973299.full_text:
🚨 EMOTIONAL STATEMENT BY AXAR PATEL ON RISHABH PANT 🚨 

Axar Patel said 🗣️ 

"First, I got a call from him [Rishabh]. And... nowadays, I spend more time with my child, so I keep my phone aside. I didn't pick up his first call. Then he called again, which I didn't pick up again. Then I called him back at night when my child slept. I said, 'What happened, anything urgent?'
He replied with, 'Bhai, it's confirmed, I am coming back.' So yes, this was our first conversation. We talked normally. Well, you're finally coming back. This was something in my mind, but I didn't say it to him. 'Subah ka bhoola, jab shaam ko ghar aaye usse bhoola nahi kehte.' But my love for him is so strong that I got him back to Delhi."

2069680674643054760.full_text:
From Rajasthan’s Corporate cricket to Ireland Cricket

- Jai Moondra used to play cricket in Rajasthan for fun. 
- He completed graduation in 2019 and faced a direct choice: stable corporate work or chasing cricket
- He gave up on cricket and moved to Ireland in 2021
- He enrolled in a Master’s programme in Electronics and Communication.
- He started playing cricket in Ireland local tournaments and started building opportunities in the local system
- His journey started in Tonk, Rajasthan, initially as a pace bowler before evolving into a batting all-rounder with left-arm spin
- That role change became important in shaping his progression and creating more value as a complete cricketer
- Playing for Leinster Cricket Club, he gradually established himself and contributed to their Irish Senior Cup success
- Now he has earned his first senior Ireland call-up for the T20I series against India. 
 - If he plays, he becomes only the second player of Asian origin to represent Ireland after Simi Singh
